items.title: Rich Paul provides an update on LeBron James’s decision

NOTE TO USER: User expressly acknowledges and agrees that, by downloading and or using this photograph, User is consenting to the terms and conditions of the Getty Images License Agreement. (Photo by Ronald Martinez/Getty Images) | Getty Images On Monday morning, Patrick Beverly made (minor) waves across the internet, stating that “rumor has it Bron makes his decision today”. While that raised a few eyebrows, it’s nothing new. We have been told enough over the last three weeks that a LeBron James free agency decision was imminent from someone supposedly in the know, only for it not to come through. Bottom line, we all still don’t know.On The Ringer’s “Game Over with Max Kellerman and Rich Paul”…

items.title: Rich Paul says LeBron James has no timetable to make free-agent decision: 'We're not going to be rushed'
items.summary: LeBron James' decision may not be as imminent as some believed. James' agent, Rich Paul, said Monday that the former Los Angeles Lakers star won't be "rushed" into picking his next team.Paul made those comments during an appearance on the "Game Over" podcast with Max Kellerman. In addition to saying James will take his time with his decision, Paul added that teams do not need to reach out to try and convince James to sign with them at this point in the process, per ESPN."There's been several people to call and say, 'Hey, Rich, is there anything more we should be doing? We could be doing?' And my answer is, 'No. We don't need you to go above and beyond about anything. We understand it. It's clear. All the messages have been sent.'"That seems to imply James has all the relevant information he needs to make his choice.…

items.title: Can the Suns tap into Miles Bridges’ two-way potential?
items.summary: On July 13, the Miles Bridges trade became official. Brian Gregory had this to say about the Suns’ new power forward.“His elite athleticism, strength and versatility allow him to impact the game on both ends of the floor. He embodies the grit, competitiveness and work ethic that fit our identity, and we’re excited to welcome him to Phoenix.”High praise. Or maybe bold praise, considering Bridges’ six-year NBA career with the Hornets.Coming out of Michigan-lite, Bridges was projected to be the type of player that Gregory is describing. A potential two-way star, with the athleticism to be a menace on the defensive end and a problem with the ball in his hands.Tom Izzo had this to say to the New York Post about Miles Bridges in advance of the 2018 draft.“I see a guy who can play a lot of positions and defend them with his strength and rebounding,’’ Izzo said. “What I’d like to see him get…

items.title: Tuesday Posted & Toasted Notes: Human Towns and pregame meals

(Photo by Mohd RASFAN / AFP) (Photo by MOHD RASFAN/AFP via Getty Images) | AFP via Getty Images Don’t start reading this with high hopes. It’s July 21, so as much as I try, it’s not that I can gather a lot of links and put together tons of notes. Bad times ahead for a workaholic like myself.Karl-Anthony Towns opened up about the emotional aftermath of winning the NBA championship during his appearance on Jay Shetty’s “On Purpose” podcast, how the achievement passed far quicker than he ever imagined, and admitted he struggled with suddenly having no basketball left to play after the Finals.Kris Pursiainen of Daily Knicks only sees one LeBron James destination that could help the Knicks, or at least not hurt them too much.Speaking of James, no decision arrived on Monday, only to shut Pat Bev’s…

items.title: LeBron James Decision 4.0 live updates: Latest rumors and team buzz
items.summary: The NBA is still waiting on LeBron James as of July 21. He's the last big domino of free agency in 2026, with league commissioner Adam Silver noting recently even the league's schedule makers need James to make a decision on his next team. James could make his pick any moment at this point, even today, and rumors of what he might do persist in the absence of clarity.The teams James is seriously considering are well-known at this point. The Cleveland Cavaliers, Miami Heat, Philadelphia 76ers and Golden State Warriors have been tabbed as favorites in multiple reports, and the Denver Nuggets and Minnesota Timberwolves have also mentioned as possibilities.James and his camp have all the information they need, according to USA TODAY Sports' Lorenzo Reyes. Now, he's just weighing out all of his options. There's nonetheless rampant speculation on when he'll be done with that process, and what…

items.title: Mavericks need consistency from Morez Johnson Jr.

(Photo by Carmen Mandato/Getty Images) | Getty Images Dallas Mavericks rookie Morez Johnson Jr. is leaving the Las Vegas Summer League with a mixed bag of tape to look over until the start of training camp.Johnson started off hot in Las Vegas but eventually cooled down, which isn’t what Mavericks fans want to see. ESPN analyst Ben Golliver listed Johnson as the most inconsistent rookie at the Las Vegas Summer League.“Johnson’s debut was a sight to behold: 27 points, eight rebounds, three assists, three steals and two blocks against the Golden State Warriors. As that line suggests, the No. 9 pick was a wrecking ball on both sides of the court.…

A lot of people think that Bird was too slow and couldn’t jump, and that he wouldn’t be able to play today. People like JJ Redick, for one, who really should know better (Redick said that he didn’t think Bird was a top-five all-time three-point shooter).…

items.title: NBA Summer League takeaways: an outstanding top four, a Warriors gem and a Cavaliers steal
items.summary: Yaxel Lendeborg helped the Warriors to the Summer League Final. Photograph: John Locher/AP1) The top four are as good as advertisedThe hype heading into this year’s draft was tremendous, and through the small sample size of 10 or so days in the Las Vegas summer heat, this year’s class has lived up to its billing.AJ Dybantsa, now a Washington Wizard, looked every bit the No 1 overall pick in his Summer League debut, where he bested the player drafted directly behind him, Darryn Peterson. This was notable, as Peterson had won the pair’s previous head to head matchups, and the noise was starting to swell that perhaps the Wizards had made the wrong selection with the No 1 pick. But Dybantsa showed his high ceiling, looking every bit the crafty and athletic offensive engine he has been touted as. Peterson, to his credit, also had a solid Summer League outing, especially in the California…
